codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original failing feature commit `4cc6919` and the fix commit `310a65a`. The fix removes Flutter's silent enum fallbacks in `apps/mobile/lib/foundation/contracts/bridge_contracts.dart:68-91` and adds regression coverage in `apps/mobile/test/foundation/contracts/bridge_contracts_test.dart:49-122`, which fully addresses the original blocking mismatch with Rust and Swift's strict enum decoding.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mobile-worker` skill example still advertises running Flutter tests from the repo root against `/repo/apps/mobile/test`, even though mission guidance requires running Flutter tests from `apps/mobile` (or through `.factory/services.yaml`) because the root-level path form can fail without `pubspec.yaml` in CWD.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mobile-worker/SKILL.md:36-41 shows `flutter test --concurrency=5 /repo/apps/mobile/test`, while mission guidance in /Users/lubomirmolin/.factory/missions/5e6e6a03-5030-4a14-862e-f91b0b989525/AGENTS.md:50-52 says to run `cd apps/mobile && flutter test ...` or use `.factory/services.yaml`.

summary: Pass. The fix feature resolves the prior blocking issue by making all Flutter shared-contract enum decoders throw on unknown wire values and by adding regression tests for invalid thread status, event kind, and access mode payloads. No new code-review issues were found, though the `mobile-worker` skill example still contains an outdated Flutter test command pattern.
